title: Fix MongoDB Projection to Handle Nested Fields with Optional Keys

statement.markdown: Implement a function that takes a MongoDB document and a projection object as input. The projection object is expected to have nested fields with optional keys. The function should return the filtered document based on the projection. If a field is not present in the document, it should be ignored in the projection. If a field has a null value, it should not be included in the projection.
statement.inputFormat: The MongoDB document is represented as a nested object, where each key is a field name and each value is a value or another nested object.
statement.outputFormat: The filtered document should also be a nested object, with the same structure as the input document but with only the fields specified in the projection.
statement.notes: The function should handle nested fields with optional keys. If a field is missing in the document, it should be ignored in the projection.

constraints.description: Number of fields in the document

hints.text: Use a recursive function to handle nested fields

editorial.title: Editorial Solution
editorial.intuition: The key insight is to use a recursive function to handle nested fields. We can iterate over the document and projection objects at the same time, using a recursive function to handle nested fields.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force solution is to manually check each field in the document and include it in the projection only if it is present in the projection object.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a recursive function to handle nested fields.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a recursive function to handle nested fields, and to ignore fields that are not present in the document.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness is by induction on the size of the document.
commonMistakes: Including fields that are not present in the document
